steps.title: Identify what would contradict the partition hypothesis
steps.content: The partition hypothesis claims the Devonian had two distinctly different biomes, each with its characteristic plant. To show this is inconsistent with Cairo evidence, the strongest finding would be that both characteristic plants coexisted at the same site during the Devonian — proving the biomes weren't actually distinct.

steps.title: Match the contradiction to the answer
steps.content:
Choice C shows exactly that — Eospermatopteris and Archaeopteris both present as contemporaneous fossils during the Devonian. This demolishes the “distinctly different” foundation; if they grew together, the biomes weren't separate. Choices A and B either support the hypothesis or address a time outside the Devonian, while D adds unrelated species without revealing whether the two key biome-plants coexisted.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
